meta.name: Pollo a la Brasa (Peruvian Rotisserie-Style Chicken)
meta.subtitle: Oven or grill method with ají-forward, smoky-garlic marinade

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalogs
notes: Designed for home ovens (wire rack + sheet pan) with optional grill and optional rotisserie notes.
Uses common Peruvian flavor markers: ají panca/ají amarillo (optional), cumin, garlic, soy + vinegar, lime.

summary: Peruvian pollo a la brasa–style chicken: deeply seasoned, smoky-garlic marinade with ají (optional), roasted hot on a rack for crisp skin and juicy meat.

yield.notes: 1 whole chicken (3.5–4.5 lb) serves 4; increase for larger birds.

signals.freezerFriendly: Cooked chicken freezes well; sauce better fresh.

heat.note: Heat depends on ají amount; keep mild by skipping spicy chiles and using ají amarillo paste sparingly.

optional.label: Rotisserie attachment
optional.notes: Optional for truer pollo a la brasa style; oven rack method works well.

optional.label: Outdoor grill
optional.notes: Optional finish for extra char and smoke.

notes: Contains soy if using soy sauce.
For gluten-free: use tamari or certified GF soy sauce.

ingredients.label: Whole chicken
ingredients.notes: Spatchcock for faster, more even cooking (recommended).

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Use 1 1/2 tsp if using fine table salt.

ingredients.label: Smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: Traditional versions may use panca for depth; smoked paprika helps mimic rotisserie smoke.

ingredients.label: Red wine vinegar
ingredients.notes: White vinegar also works; red wine vinegar is smoother.

ingredients.label: Soy sauce (or tamari)
ingredients.notes: Optional but strongly recommended for color and savoriness.

ingredients.label: Ají panca paste
ingredients.notes: Adds deep, fruity, mildly spicy chile flavor.

ingredients.label: Ají amarillo paste
ingredients.notes: Adds brighter heat; increase to 1 tbsp for spicier.

ingredients.label: Dijon mustard
ingredients.notes: Optional emulsifier; many home adaptations use it.

ingredients.label: Baking powder
ingredients.notes: Optional: boosts crisp skin; use aluminum-free if possible.

steps.title: Prep the chicken
instructions: If possible, spatchcock the chicken (remove backbone, flatten). This speeds cooking and crisps the skin evenly.
Pat the chicken very dry with paper towels.
Optional (best results): lightly loosen skin over breasts and thighs with your fingers to help marinade reach the meat.
checks: Chicken is dry on the surface (helps browning).

steps.title: Mix the Peruvian-style marinade
instructions: In a bowl, combine salt, pepper, cumin, oregano, smoked paprika, garlic, lime juice, vinegar, soy sauce, and oil.
Optional Peruvian boosts: whisk in ají panca and/or ají amarillo paste.
Optional: add Dijon mustard to help emulsify.
Optional crisp-skin boost: whisk in baking powder (do not use baking soda).
checks: Marinade is a thick, brushable paste.

steps.title: Marinate (quick or overnight)
instructions: Rub marinade all over chicken, including under loosened skin if you did that step.
For best flavor: refrigerate uncovered (or loosely covered) 8–24 hours on a rack over a tray.
For same-day: marinate at least 30 minutes; 2–4 hours is better.
checks: If chilling overnight: chicken is on a rack for airflow.

steps.title: Preheat oven and set up roasting rack
instructions: Preheat oven to 450°F (232°C). Place a rack in the upper-middle position.
Set a wire rack on a rimmed baking sheet (line sheet with foil for easy cleanup).
Place chicken skin-side up on the rack. Let it sit at room temperature 15–20 minutes while oven finishes heating.
checks: Chicken elevated on rack (airflow = crisp skin).

steps.title: Roast until deeply browned and cooked through
instructions: Roast at 450°F (232°C) for 20 minutes to set the skin and start browning.
Reduce oven to 375°F (190°C) and continue roasting until done.
Target internal temperature: 160–165°F (71–74°C) in the thickest breast and 175–185°F (79–85°C) in the thigh.
If skin needs more color near the end, broil 1–3 minutes, watching closely.
checks: Skin is deeply golden-brown with some darker edges.
Juices run clear at thigh joint.

steps.title: Rest, carve, and serve
instructions: Rest chicken 10–15 minutes before carving (carryover heat finishes the cook).
Carve into quarters (or slice breast and separate thighs/drumsticks).
Serve with ají verde, fries, roasted potatoes, or a simple lime-onion salad.
checks: Carve cleanly; juices stay in the meat.

refrigerate.notes: Cool leftovers quickly; refrigerate within 2 hours.

freeze.notes: Freeze cooked chicken portions tightly wrapped; best within 2 months for quality.

reheat.notes: Reheat in 350°F oven until hot (or air fryer) to re-crisp skin; microwave softens skin.

leftoverUses: chicken bowls with lime and ají verde
tacos or wraps with onion + cilantro

notes: No ají pastes? Use smoked paprika + a small pinch of cayenne (optional) and add more garlic + lime; it won’t be identical but still excellent.
Spatchcocking is the single biggest upgrade for home ovens: faster cook, more crisp skin, more even doneness.
Marinate as written, then cook thighs/drumsticks at 380°F for ~20–25 min, breasts ~16–20 min, flipping halfway.
Cook to internal temps listed; times vary by size.
Roast until 150°F in breast, then finish over medium-high grill heat for char (skin-side down first), then rest.
Avoid cross-contamination: keep raw chicken separate; sanitize board/knife/hands.
If marinating overnight, keep refrigerated below 40°F (4°C).
